segments.title: Soviet Navy as a tool for winning the peace
segments.summary: Dr. Clarke argues that the late Soviet Navy's core value was political reach, status, and alliance pressure rather than pure warfighting.
segments.body: Soviet surface raiders, global presence, and naval procurement made the USSR look capable worldwide and prevented NATO from taking non-Western partners for granted.

evidence.note: The closing argument says the Soviet Navy's key contribution was making the USSR look powerful, capable, and useful to allies around the world.
The conclusion restates the winning-the-peace thesis and links Soviet naval strategy to procurement choices and capability signals.
